name: Pumpkin, Leek & Goat Cheese Gratin
recipeInstructions:
or 1 cup each milk and cream for a richer gratin
Preheat oven to 375°F. Peel the pumpkin, quarter it, remove seeds and strings. Slice into ¼-inch thick "planks" and set aside.
Trim the leeks, removing tough outer leaves and washing free of all dirt. Slice across in thin julienne strips.
Peel, halve and julienne the onion, and slice the garlic thinly.
Heat olive oil in a skillet; sauté the leeks, onion, and garlic until tender. Season with salt and pepper.
In an oiled 10-inch gratin dish, scatter some of the leek mixture around. Evenly layer pumpkin slices over the leeks, completely covering the surface. Scatter more of the leek mixture over the pumpkin and dot with crumbled goat cheese. Season with salt, pepper, and chopped thyme leaves. Moisten with milk.
Repeat layers until the dish is filled; then add enough milk to submerge the ingredients completely.
Cover and bake on a sheet for about 30 minutes or until the pumpkin is tender. Carefully pour off excess milk and dot the surface of the gratin with more goat cheese. Place under the broiler for a few minutes until lightly browned.
